sections.paragraph:
περιγίνομαι aor. 3 sg. περιεγένετο (TestIss 3:5); pass. aor. ptc. fem. acc. sg. περιγενηθεῖσαν 1 Ch 28:19 (Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; En 102:6; Test12Patr; Philo, Op. M. 155; Tat. 11, 2; T. Kellis 22, 97) to prevail over, overcome w. gen. (Hdt., Aristoph. et al.; Aelian, VH 1, 3; Vett. Val. p. 38, 20; 4 Macc 13:3; Jos., Ant. 7, 165) Hv 1, 3, 2.—DELG s.v. γίγνομαι.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

acc. acc. = accusative

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

  1. Kellis 22 T. Kellis 22 = RJenkins, The Prayer of the Emanations in Greek from Kellis (inscribed on a tablet)—List 3

gen. gen. = genitive

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 800
